what_it_does: Defines a fixed 1600×900 HTML/CSS presentation showing two side-by-side cards for warm and cool surface tones, with metric boxes, short descriptive fragments, a faint blueprint grid, a gold-wash summary band, footer texture, and circular mark.
observations: The document is a standalone static HTML page with all layout styling embedded in a style block.
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and a root-relative /surface/styles/vars.css stylesheet.
The main canvas is .view, fixed at 1600px by 900px and centered in the body.
The title reads "True tones, straight from the source."
The main content is a two-column grid of cards labeled warm plate and cool plate.
Both cards use repeated stat rows with nested boxes, labels, divider rules, and triangular bullet markers.
The warm card uses gold numeric emphasis; the cool card uses charcoal numeric emphasis and bronze triangle markers.
A low-opacity blueprint-like grid sits in the upper right with a radial mask.
The bottom band uses var(--goldwash), a faint diagonal line overlay, a simple CSS clock icon, and explanatory text.
The :root block contains comments about sampled values but no custom property declarations.
standouts: The page is organized around direct visual comparison of var(--warm) and var(--cool).
Surface separation is expressed through borders, shadows, gaps, and nested boxes rather than dark fills.
The gold-wash summary band reinforces the color-token sampling theme.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for color, spacing, type, and radius tokens.
Uses semantic-light class naming tied to visual roles such as .card, .statrow, .box, .band, and .plan.
Keeps the composition as a fixed artboard rather than a responsive document.
Uses CSS-generated decorative elements for the blueprint grid, clock, diagonal band texture, triangles, footer texture, and circular mark.
concerns: The :root block comments imply sampled surface declarations, but no values are declared there.
Several required custom properties are referenced without file-local fallbacks.
The layout is fixed to 1600×900 and may not adapt outside that canvas.
The intended rendering depends on external font and stylesheet availability.
notes: A static tone-study slide comparing warm and cool plate surfaces with token-labeled cards and a subtle gold summary band.
questions: Are the omitted sampled custom property values intentionally supplied only by /surface/styles/vars.css?
Is this intended purely as a fixed slide/artboard rather than a responsive page?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered content: it presents true-tone warm and cool cards using sampled/tokenized surface colors, though the sampled values themselves are not declared in this file.
intention_signals: It appears intended to demonstrate how source-sampled warm, cool, nested, and gold-wash surfaces behave together in a controlled two-card layout.
novelty: The distinct element is the direct warm/cool plate comparison paired with intentionally low-contrast surface separation and token labels embedded in the visible card headings.
contribution: Contributes a fixed-slide visual substrate for comparing warm and cool surface tokens, including paired card anatomy, nested metric boxes, faint technical-plan texture, and a subdued gold summary band treatment.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed 1600×900 HTML/CSS tone-study slide with two token-labeled warm and cool cards, nested metric boxes, soft borders/shadows, blueprint texture, and a gold-wash summary band.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting sampled true-tone surface values in a neutral two-card comparison that emphasizes hue cast, lightness, and soft separation.
